content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

In one operation, Chef can choose any subsequence of the array and any integer XXX and then add XXX to **all** the elements of the chosen subsequence.

Determine the **minimum** number of operations required to make all the elements of the array **distinct**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of Array AAA.
Next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​ - denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of operations required to make all the elements distinct.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Operation 111: Choose the subsequence {A1}\{A_1\}{A1​} and add X=1X = 1X=1 to the elements. The array becomes A=[4,3,3]A = [4, 3, 3]A=[4,3,3].
Operation 222: Choose the subsequence {A2}\{A_2\}{A2​} and add X=2X = 2X=2 to the elements. The array becomes A=[4,5,3]A = [4, 5, 3]A=[4,5,3].

Thus, we require, minimum 222 operations to make all the elements distinct.
Test case 222:

Operation 111: Choose the subsequence {A1,A6}\{A_1, A_6\}{A1​,A6​} and add X=4X = 4X=4 to the elements. The array becomes A=[5,3,2,1,2,6]A = [5, 3, 2, 1, 2, 6]A=[5,3,2,1,2,6].
Operation 222: Choose the subsequence {A3}\{A_3\}{A3​} and add X=5X = 5X=5 to the elements. The array becomes A=[5,3,7,1,2,6]A = [5, 3, 7, 1, 2, 6]A=[5,3,7,1,2,6].

Thus, we require, minimum 222 operations to make all the elements distinct.
Test case 333:

Operation 111: Choose the subsequence {A3,A4}\{A_3, A_4\}{A3​,A4​} and add X=2X = 2X=2 to the elements. The array becomes A=[1,2,3,4]A = [1, 2, 3, 4]A=[1,2,3,4].

Thus, we require, minimum 111 operation to make all the elements distinct.
Test case 444: All the elements are distinct. We need zero operations.
